subject: Electrical
question_text: A 12 V battery is connected across a 4 Ω resistor. The current through the circuit is:
explanation: I=V/R=12/4=3 A (Ohm's Law).

subject: Electrical
question_text: Three resistors of 6 Ω, 3 Ω and 2 Ω are connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: Power dissipated in a 10 Ω resistor carrying 2 A current is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law states that the algebraic sum of all voltages in a closed loop is:
explanation: KVL: ΣV=0 around any closed loop (energy conservation).

subject: Electrical
question_text: A 100 W, 250 V bulb and a 60 W, 250 V bulb are connected in series across 250 V. Which bulb glows brighter?
explanation: In series, same current flows. R(60W)=250²/60≈1042Ω > R(100W)=250²/100=625Ω. Higher resistance → more voltage → more power → 60W bulb glows brighter.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The unit of electrical resistance is:
explanation: Resistance is measured in Ohms (Ω). Named after Georg Simon Ohm.

subject: Electrical
question_text: In a series circuit of R₁=5Ω and R₂=10Ω connected to 30V, voltage across R₂ is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: The frequency of AC supply in India is:
explanation: India uses 50 Hz AC supply. USA/Canada use 60 Hz.

subject: Electrical
question_text: RMS value of a sinusoidal AC voltage with peak value Vm is:
explanation: VRMS=Vm/√2≈0.707Vm for pure sinusoidal waveform.

subject: Electrical
question_text: In a purely inductive AC circuit, current lags voltage by:
explanation: In a pure inductor, current lags voltage by 90° (π/2 radians).

subject: Electrical
question_text: Power factor of a purely resistive circuit is:
explanation: PF=cosφ. For purely resistive load, φ=0°, PF=cos0°=1.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A series RLC circuit has R=10Ω, XL=20Ω, XC=10Ω. The impedance Z is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: The standard domestic single-phase voltage in India is:
explanation: Single-phase domestic supply in India: 230V (±6%), 50Hz.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A transformer with primary turns 200 and secondary turns 400, primary voltage 230V. Secondary voltage is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: Transformer cores are made of laminated silicon steel to reduce:
explanation: Lamination increases resistance to eddy currents, reducing eddy current losses (P∝t², where t=lamination thickness).

subject: Electrical
question_text: A step-up transformer increases:
explanation: Step-up transformer increases voltage while decreasing current (power stays constant, ignoring losses).

subject: Electrical
question_text: A transformer has 100% efficiency with input 1000W. If primary current is 5A at 200V, secondary voltage is 400V. Secondary current is:
explanation: Power out=Power in=1000W. I₂=P/V₂=1000/400=2.5A.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Transformer oil is used for:
explanation: Transformer oil (mineral oil) provides insulation and carries away heat from windings and core.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The speed of a 4-pole induction motor on 50 Hz supply (synchronous speed) is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: The slip of a 3-phase induction motor running at 1440 rpm with synchronous speed 1500 rpm is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: A DC motor has back EMF because:
options: Armature rotates in magnetic field generating EMF opposing supply
explanation: When armature rotates, it cuts magnetic flux and generates a back EMF (Eb) opposing the applied voltage (V). Eb=V−IaRa.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The rotor of a squirrel cage induction motor is made of:
explanation: Squirrel cage rotor: conducting bars (Al or Cu) embedded in slots, short-circuited by end rings — no external connections.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Which starting method for a 3-phase induction motor gives the lowest starting current?
explanation: Star-Delta reduces starting voltage by 1/√3, current by 1/3 of DOL current — lowest among mechanical starters.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A voltmeter is always connected:
explanation: Voltmeter measures potential difference — must be in parallel to avoid disturbing circuit.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Megger is used to measure:
explanation: Megger (Megohmmeter) measures insulation resistance in MΩ — used to test winding insulation of motors/cables.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A wattmeter measures:
explanation: Wattmeter has current coil (series) and pressure coil (parallel) — deflection∝V×I×cosφ = real power (W).

subject: Electrical
question_text: The instrument used to measure insulation resistance between two conductors is:
explanation: Megger applies high DC voltage (500V/1000V) to measure insulation resistance in MΩ.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The colour code of the earth wire in Indian standard wiring is:
explanation: IS 694: Earth=Green/Yellow, Phase=Red/Yellow/Blue (3-ph), Neutral=Black.

subject: Electrical
question_text: An ELCB (Earth Leakage Circuit Breaker) trips when leakage current exceeds:
explanation: Standard ELCB/RCCB trips at 30 mA leakage current — this level can cause ventricular fibrillation.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The purpose of earthing in electrical installations is to:
options: Provide low resistance path for fault current
explanation: Earthing channels fault current safely to ground, operating the protective device and preventing electric shock.

subject: Electrical
question_text: MCB stands for:
explanation: MCB (Miniature Circuit Breaker) automatically breaks the circuit on overload or short circuit.

subject: Electrical
question_text: EMF of a fully charged lead-acid cell is approximately:
explanation: Fully charged lead-acid cell: 2.1V. A 12V battery has 6 cells (6×2.1=12.6V).

subject: Electrical
question_text: During discharge of a lead-acid battery, the specific gravity of electrolyte:
explanation: H₂SO₄ is consumed during discharge → specific gravity decreases (from ~1.28 charged to ~1.15 discharged).

subject: Electrical
question_text: The electrolyte used in a lead-acid battery is:
explanation: Lead-acid battery uses dilute H₂SO₄ (sulphuric acid) as electrolyte.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A diode allows current flow:
explanation: A PN junction diode conducts in forward bias (P+, N−) and blocks in reverse bias.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A full-wave bridge rectifier converts AC to DC using:
explanation: Bridge rectifier uses 4 diodes in a bridge arrangement to use both half-cycles of AC.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The device used to regulate output voltage in a power supply is:
explanation: Zener diode maintains constant voltage in reverse breakdown region — used as voltage regulator.

subject: Electrical
question_text: NPN transistor operates when base is:
explanation: NPN transistor: B-E junction forward biased, B-C junction reverse biased for active operation.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The ripple frequency of a full-wave rectifier on 50 Hz supply is:
explanation: Full-wave rectifier rectifies both half-cycles → ripple frequency = 2×supply frequency = 2×50=100 Hz.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A Wheatstone bridge has arms P=100Ω, Q=150Ω, R=200Ω. For balance, S must be:
explanation: Balance condition: P/Q = R/S → S = QR/P = 150×200/100 = 300 Ω.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Norton's theorem replaces a network with:
options: A current source in parallel with resistance
A voltage source in series with resistance
A current source in series with resistance
explanation: Norton equivalent = Norton current (Iₙ) in parallel with Norton resistance (Rₙ). Dual of Thevenin.

subject: Electrical
question_text: In a series RLC circuit at resonance, the impedance is:
explanation: At resonance XL = XC, so reactive components cancel. Z = R (purely resistive, minimum impedance).

subject: Electrical
question_text: The power factor of a purely inductive circuit is:
explanation: In a purely inductive circuit, current lags voltage by 90°. PF = cos 90° = 0 (lagging).

subject: Electrical
question_text: A 50 Hz AC circuit has XL = 314 Ω. The inductance L is approximately:

subject: Electrical
question_text: A transformer has 500 primary turns and 2000 secondary turns. If primary voltage is 230 V, secondary voltage is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: Core loss in a transformer is measured by:
explanation: Open circuit test (LV side energised, HV side open) measures iron/core losses at rated voltage and frequency.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The efficiency of a transformer is maximum when:
explanation: Max efficiency condition: variable copper loss = constant iron loss. This gives maximum η.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The speed of a DC shunt motor under no-load condition compared to full-load is:
explanation: At no load, back EMF ≈ supply voltage, flux is nearly constant, so speed is slightly higher than full load.

subject: Electrical
question_text: In a 3-phase induction motor, slip at full load is typically:
explanation: At full load, 3-phase induction motors have slip between 2–5%. At synchronous speed, slip=0; at standstill, slip=1.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The synchronous speed of a 4-pole induction motor at 50 Hz supply is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: A moving coil instrument reads correctly on:
explanation: Moving coil (PMMC) instruments use permanent magnets and respond to mean current — DC only. AC gives zero deflection.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A shunt is used with an ammeter to:
explanation: Shunt is a low-resistance path in parallel with the ammeter, allowing most current to bypass the meter movement.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The dielectric strength of air at standard conditions is approximately:
explanation: Air breakdown voltage is approximately 3 kV/mm (3 MV/m) at STP. This determines insulation design in air-gap equipment.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The per-unit impedance of a transformer does NOT change with change in:
explanation: Per-unit (pu) value of transformer impedance is the same whether referred to primary or secondary — this is a key advantage of pu system.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A system has characteristic equation s³ + 2s² + 4s + 8 = 0. Using Routh-Hurwitz, the system is:
explanation: Routh array: Row1: 1,4; Row2: 2,8; Row3: (2×4-1×8)/2=0. A zero row indicates marginal stability.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The steady-state error for a ramp input applied to a Type-1 system is:
explanation: Type-1 system has one integrator. For ramp input, ess = 1/Kv where Kv is velocity constant — finite, non-zero.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A Zener diode is primarily used as:
explanation: Zener diode operates in reverse breakdown at a fixed voltage — used as voltage reference/regulator.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The current gain (β) of a transistor in CE configuration is 100. Its α in CB configuration is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: Two wattmeters W1 = 2000 W and W2 = 500 W in a 3-phase balanced load measurement. The power factor is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: The torque developed by a DC motor is proportional to:
explanation: T ∝ Φ × Ia. DC motor torque equation: T = (P/2πA) × Φ × Ia.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Corona discharge on power lines is reduced by:
explanation: Corona occurs when electric field at conductor surface exceeds critical disruptive voltage. Increasing diameter reduces surface field gradient.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The creeping error in an energy meter is reduced by:
options: Drilling two small holes in the disc
explanation: Two small diametrically opposite holes in the aluminium disc stop it from rotating continuously at no-load, preventing creeping.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Three 6 Ω resistors are connected in delta. The equivalent star resistance of each arm is:
explanation: Delta to Star: Rstar = Rdelta/3 = 6/3 = 2 Ω (for balanced delta).

subject: Electrical
question_text: A full-wave bridge rectifier with no filter has a ripple factor of approximately:
explanation: Ripple factor for full-wave rectifier = 0.482 ≈ 0.48. Half-wave ≈ 1.21.

subject: Electrical
question_text: In a parallel RLC circuit at resonance, the impedance is:
explanation: Parallel RLC at resonance: susceptances cancel (BL=BC), leaving only conductance. Z = R (maximum impedance, minimum current).

subject: Electrical
question_text: The Q-factor of a series RLC circuit is 10, bandwidth is 100 Hz. The resonant frequency is:
explanation: Q = f₀/BW → f₀ = Q × BW = 10 × 100 = 1000 Hz.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The starting torque of a squirrel cage induction motor compared to slip ring motor is:
explanation: Squirrel cage rotors have fixed low resistance. Slip ring motors allow external resistance addition, improving starting torque. So SCIM has lower starting torque.

subject: Electrical
question_text: In a DC generator, the function of a commutator is to:
explanation: The armature EMF is alternating; the commutator acts as a mechanical rectifier, converting the internal AC to DC at the output terminals.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Buchholz relay is used for protection of:
explanation: Buchholz relay is an oil-and-gas-actuated protection relay fitted in the pipe between the transformer tank and the conservator. Protects oil-cooled transformers.

subject: Electrical
question_text: ACSR conductor stands for:
explanation: ACSR = Aluminium Conductor Steel Reinforced. Outer strands are aluminium (high conductivity), inner core is steel (high tensile strength).

subject: Electrical
question_text: The surge impedance of a lossless transmission line is:
explanation: Surge impedance Z₀ = √(L/C) where L is inductance per unit length and C is capacitance per unit length of the line.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The transfer function of an ideal differentiator is:
explanation: Differentiator output = d(input)/dt. In Laplace domain, differentiation is multiplication by s. Transfer function G(s) = s.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Phase margin of a stable system is:
explanation: Phase margin = 180° + ∠G(jω) at gain crossover. For stable system, phase margin must be positive (typically 30°–60° for good design).

subject: Electrical
question_text: An SCR (Silicon Controlled Rectifier) can be turned off by:
options: Reducing anode current below holding current
Applying negative anode voltage momentarily then restoring
explanation: Once triggered, SCR latches ON. To turn it off: reduce anode current below holding current (natural commutation) or force commutation. A gate signal cannot turn it off.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The pinch-off voltage of a JFET is the gate-source voltage at which:
options: Channel is completely depleted and drain current is zero
explanation: At pinch-off voltage (Vp), the depletion region extends across the entire channel, reducing drain current to zero (in the transfer characteristic).

subject: Electrical
question_text: A 0–100 V voltmeter has 1000 Ω/V sensitivity. Its internal resistance is:
explanation: Internal resistance = sensitivity × full scale voltage = 1000 × 100 = 100,000 Ω = 100 kΩ.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The maximum power transfer theorem states that maximum power is delivered when load resistance equals:
explanation: For DC circuits, max power transfer: RL = RTH. Max power = VTH²/(4RTH). Efficiency at max power transfer is 50%.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The form factor of a sinusoidal wave is:
explanation: Form factor = RMS/Average = (Vm/√2)/(2Vm/π) = π/(2√2) ≈ 1.11. Peak factor = Vm/Vrms = √2 ≈ 1.414.

subject: Electrical
question_text: String efficiency of an insulator string is improved by:
explanation: String efficiency = V/(n×Vn). Grading (using different capacitance discs or a grading ring) equalises voltage distribution, improving string efficiency.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The speed regulation of an ideal DC shunt generator is:
explanation: DC shunt motor (and generator) provides approximately constant speed due to self-regulating flux — good speed regulation compared to series motor.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Reactive power in an AC circuit is measured in:
explanation: Reactive power Q = VIsinφ, measured in VAR. Active power P in Watts, apparent power S in VA, energy in Wh or kWh.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) is based on conservation of:
explanation: KCL: sum of currents at a node = 0 (Σi=0). This is based on the principle of conservation of charge — charge cannot accumulate at a node.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The percentage regulation of an ideal transformer is:
explanation: Ideal transformer has no internal impedance drop. Voltage regulation = (VNL-VFL)/VFL × 100 = 0% since VNL = VFL.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The output characteristic of a Common Emitter transistor shows collector current versus:
explanation: CE output characteristic: IC vs VCE family of curves, each curve at a fixed base current IB. Used to determine operating point and load line.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The load factor of a power system is defined as:
explanation: Load factor = Average load / Maximum demand. Always ≤ 1. Higher load factor means better utilisation of installed capacity.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A system with transfer function G(s) = 1/(s+1) is a:
explanation: G(s) = 1/(s+1) = 1/(τs+1) with τ=1 — first-order system. Its step response is exponential with time constant 1 second.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Which instrument is used to measure insulation resistance?
explanation: Megger (Megohmmeter) applies a high DC voltage (500V, 1kV, 2.5kV) and measures insulation resistance in MΩ — used for cables, motors, transformers.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The time constant of an RL series circuit with R=100Ω and L=0.5H is:
explanation: τ = L/R = 0.5/100 = 0.005 s = 5 ms. After one time constant, current reaches 63.2% of its final value.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The power factor at which a synchronous motor operates can be controlled by varying:
explanation: Synchronous motor is unique — its PF can be controlled by varying field excitation. Over-excited → leading PF; under-excited → lagging PF. Used as synchronous condenser.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Three resistors of 4Ω, 6Ω, 12Ω are connected in parallel. Equivalent resistance is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: A battery of EMF 12 V and internal resistance 1Ω drives a 5Ω load. Terminal voltage is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: The RMS value of a sinusoidal voltage with peak value 100 V is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: Inductive reactance (XL) of a 0.2 H inductor at 50 Hz is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: A step-down transformer has turns ratio 10:1. If primary is 2200 V, secondary voltage is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: A 4-pole DC motor has 800 conductors, flux per pole 0.02 Wb, speed 1500 rpm, wave winding. Back EMF is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: The slip of an induction motor at no load is approximately:
explanation: At no load, the motor runs near synchronous speed. Slip s = (Ns-N)/Ns ≈ 0 (typically 0.001–0.005).

subject: Electrical
question_text: An ammeter is always connected in:
explanation: Ammeter measures current and must be in series so the same current flows through it. It has very low resistance.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A voltmeter has very high internal resistance so that it:
options: Draws negligible current from the circuit
explanation: High resistance ensures the voltmeter draws negligible current, so it doesn't significantly disturb the measured circuit.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Ohm's law states that current through a conductor is:
options: Directly proportional to voltage at constant temperature
explanation: Ohm's law: V = IR → I = V/R. At constant temperature, I ∝ V for ohmic conductors.

subject: Electrical
question_text: In a p-n junction diode, forward bias causes the depletion region to:
explanation: Forward bias reduces the potential barrier, narrowing the depletion region and allowing majority carrier diffusion (forward current).

subject: Electrical
question_text: The ripple frequency of a full-wave rectifier (supply frequency f) is:
explanation: Full-wave rectifier produces two peaks per cycle of supply. Ripple frequency = 2f (100 Hz for 50 Hz supply).

subject: Electrical
question_text: Power factor of a circuit is defined as:
options: cos φ (ratio of active to apparent power)
explanation: PF = cos φ = P/S = Active Power / Apparent Power. Unity PF means all power is active (resistive load).

subject: Electrical
question_text: Capacitive reactance (XC) of a 100 μF capacitor at 50 Hz is approximately:

subject: Electrical
question_text: A closed-loop control system uses feedback to:
options: Reduce error between desired and actual output
explanation: Negative feedback in closed-loop systems continuously compares output to reference and corrects the error, improving accuracy and stability.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The starting method that gives the highest starting torque for a squirrel cage induction motor is:
explanation: DOL starting applies full voltage directly → full starting current and maximum torque. Star-Delta and autotransformer reduce voltage and thus reduce starting torque.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Which losses in a transformer are constant regardless of load?
explanation: Core losses (hysteresis + eddy current) depend only on voltage and frequency — constant at all loads. Copper losses (I²R) vary with load current.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The unit of electrical conductance is:
explanation: Conductance G = 1/R. Unit is Siemens (S) = 1/Ω. Previously called 'mho' (Ω spelled backwards).

subject: Electrical
question_text: The output of a half-wave rectifier has an average (DC) value of Vm/π. For peak voltage 100 V, average DC is approximately:
explanation: Vavg = Vm/π = 100/3.14 ≈ 31.8 V. Full-wave: Vavg = 2Vm/π ≈ 63.7 V.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The colour of the neutral wire in Indian wiring standards (IS:3043) is:
explanation: Old Indian standard: Red=Phase, Black=Neutral, Green=Earth. New IS 694 (IEC aligned): Brown=Phase, Blue=Neutral, Green-Yellow=Earth.

subject: Electrical
question_text: In a purely resistive AC circuit, voltage and current are:
explanation: Pure resistance: V and I are in phase (φ=0). Power factor = cos 0° = 1. All power is active.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The speed of a DC series motor at no load is:
explanation: DC series motor: flux ∝ Ia. At no load, Ia→0, flux→0, N = E/Φ → dangerously high. Series motors must never be run unloaded.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The full deflection of a galvanometer gives 1 mA. To convert it to a 5 A ammeter, the shunt resistance (galvanometer resistance 100 Ω) is:

subject: Electrical
question_text: An integrator in a control system has transfer function:
explanation: Integration in time domain corresponds to 1/s in Laplace domain. An integrator accumulates error over time.

subject: Electrical
question_text: MCB stands for:
explanation: MCB = Miniature Circuit Breaker. It provides overcurrent and short-circuit protection for low-voltage circuits and trips automatically on fault.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A 3-phase fully controlled converter with firing angle α=60° and DC output voltage Vdc=? (Vs line=400V):
explanation: Vdc = (3√6/π)×Vs_phase×cos α = (3√2/π)×Vs_line×cos 60°/... For 3φ full converter: Vdc = (3√6/π)×(400/√3)×cos60° ≈ 270 V.

subject: Electrical
question_text: A chopper (DC-DC converter) has duty cycle D=0.6, input voltage 100V. Average output voltage is:
explanation: Step-down (buck) chopper: Vo = D×Vi = 0.6×100 = 60 V.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The condition for maximum power transfer in DC motor occurs at back EMF:
explanation: Power = Eb×Ia = Eb×(V-Eb)/Ra. dP/dEb = 0 → V-2Eb=0 → Eb=V/2. Max power at half supply voltage, but efficiency=50% only.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The number of roots with positive real parts for characteristic equation s³+2s²+s+2=0 (by Routh):
explanation: Routh: Row1:1,1; Row2:2,2; Row3:(2×1-1×2)/2=0 → row of zeros → marginal stability. Auxiliary equation: 2s²+2=0 → s=±j → imaginary roots.

subject: Electrical
question_text: In a 3-phase star-connected system with line voltage 415V, the phase voltage is:
explanation: Star: Vphase = Vline/√3 = 415/1.732 ≈ 239.6 V.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Cogging in an induction motor occurs due to:
options: Magnetic locking between stator and rotor slot harmonics
explanation: Cogging (magnetic locking): when number of stator and rotor slots have common factor, flux harmonics create reluctance torques causing motor to refuse to start.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The charging current in a high-voltage cable is due to:
explanation: Underground HV cables have significant capacitance (conductor−dielectric−sheath). Charging current Ic = V×ωC flows even at no load, affecting power factor.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The ballistic galvanometer is used to measure:
explanation: Ballistic galvanometer measures charge (impulse of current). The first deflection (throw) is proportional to total charge, not steady current.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The Miller effect in amplifiers increases the effective input capacitance by a factor of:
explanation: Miller capacitance: Cin_eff = C(1+A). High-gain inverting amplifiers (like CE BJT or common-source FET) suffer from Miller effect, reducing bandwidth.

subject: Electrical
question_text: IGBT (Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistor) combines characteristics of:
explanation: IGBT = MOSFET input (voltage-controlled, high impedance gate) + BJT output (low saturation voltage). Used in inverters, motor drives, UPS for medium-high power.

subject: Electrical
question_text: The damping ratio ζ=0 corresponds to:
explanation: ζ=0: undamped (pure oscillation); ζ<1: underdamped; ζ=1: critically damped; ζ>1: overdamped. Poles on imaginary axis for ζ=0.

subject: Electrical
question_text: Skin effect in conductors causes current to flow mainly:
options: On the outer surface at high frequencies
explanation: Skin effect: at high frequency, current concentrates on conductor surface within skin depth δ = √(2ρ/ωμ). Increases effective AC resistance beyond DC resistance.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Ohm's law states that current is:
explanation: V=IR so I=V/R. At constant R, I∝V (Ohm's law).

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: The unit of electrical resistance is:
explanation: Resistance is measured in Ohms (Ω), named after Georg Simon Ohm.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: A conductor of 4Ω is connected to 12V. Current through it is:

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Power consumed in a resistor R carrying current I is:
explanation: P=VI=I²R=V²/R. Most common form: P=I²R.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Total resistance of 3Ω and 6Ω in parallel is:

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Total resistance of 3Ω and 6Ω in series is:

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: 1 kilowatt-hour (kWh) is equal to how many joules?

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Which material is the best conductor of electricity?
explanation: Silver has the highest electrical conductivity (6.30×10⁷ S/m), though copper is more used due to cost.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: EMF stands for:
explanation: EMF = Electromotive Force, the energy per unit charge supplied by a source (measured in Volts).

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: The function of a fuse in a circuit is to:
explanation: A fuse melts when current exceeds rated value, breaking the circuit and protecting equipment.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: In India, the standard AC supply frequency is:
explanation: India uses 50 Hz AC supply. The USA uses 60 Hz.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Peak voltage of 230V RMS AC supply is approximately:

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: In a purely resistive AC circuit, current and voltage are:
explanation: In a pure resistor, voltage and current are in phase (phase angle φ=0°).

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: The unit of frequency is:
explanation: Frequency is the number of cycles per second, measured in Hertz (Hz). 1 Hz = 1 cycle/second.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: A transformer works on the principle of:
explanation: A transformer uses Faraday's law of mutual induction — changing flux in primary induces EMF in secondary.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: In an ideal step-up transformer, the secondary voltage is:
explanation: Step-up transformer: Ns>Np → Vs>Vp. Vs/Vp = Ns/Np.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: The core of a transformer is made of:
explanation: Laminated silicon steel reduces eddy current losses in the core.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: A DC generator converts:
explanation: A generator converts mechanical energy (rotation) to electrical energy via electromagnetic induction.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: The speed of an induction motor is:
explanation: Induction motors run at slightly below synchronous speed (slip > 0) — the slip creates the relative motion needed for torque.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Slip in an induction motor is defined as:
explanation: Slip s=(Ns−N)/Ns where Ns=synchronous speed, N=actual speed. At full load, s≈3–5%.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Earth wire in domestic wiring is usually coloured:
explanation: IS standards: Earth = Green/Yellow, Live/Phase = Red (old) or Brown (new), Neutral = Black or Blue.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: An ELCB (Earth Leakage Circuit Breaker) trips when:
explanation: ELCB detects leakage current to earth (≥30 mA) and trips within 30ms to prevent electric shock.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Which of the following is a good insulator?
explanation: Rubber is an electrical insulator — used in cable sheaths, gloves, and boot soles for safety.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: A voltmeter is connected:
explanation: Voltmeter measures potential difference across a component — it must be in parallel. It has very high internal resistance.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: An ammeter is connected:
explanation: Ammeter measures current flow — it must be in series. It has very low internal resistance.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: A multimeter can measure:
explanation: A multimeter combines voltmeter, ammeter, and ohmmeter functions, plus continuity check.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Charge on one electron is approximately:
explanation: Elementary charge e=1.6×10⁻¹⁹ Coulombs. This is the fundamental unit of charge.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: The relation between charge Q, current I and time t is:
explanation: Charge Q=I×t. Current is the rate of flow of charge: I=Q/t.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: PVC in electrical wiring refers to:
explanation: PVC (Polyvinyl Chloride) is the most common insulation material in domestic wiring.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: The standard household supply voltage in India is:
explanation: Single-phase domestic supply in India is 230V AC at 50 Hz. Three-phase is 415V (line-to-line).

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Which switch disconnects all poles simultaneously?
explanation: A double pole (DP) switch breaks both live and neutral simultaneously — used for safety isolation.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: The RMS value of a sinusoidal AC current with peak value Im is:
explanation: IRMS=Im/√2≈0.707Im. RMS gives equivalent DC heating effect.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: 1 unit of electrical energy consumed is:
explanation: 1 unit = 1 kWh (kilowatt-hour) — the standard unit for billing electrical energy.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: The function of commutator in a DC motor is to:
explanation: Commutator + brushes convert the internally generated AC into DC at the output terminals.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) states that:
options: Sum of currents at a node = 0
explanation: KCL: ΣI = 0 at any node (sum of incoming = sum of outgoing currents). Conservation of charge.

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: The magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor is described by:
explanation: Ampere's law: The line integral of B around a closed path = μ₀×(enclosed current).

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Power factor of a purely inductive circuit is:
explanation: In a pure inductor, current lags voltage by 90°. pf=cos(90°)=0 (lagging).

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: Resonance in a series RLC circuit occurs when:
explanation: At resonance, inductive reactance XL = capacitive reactance XC. Net reactance=0, impedance=R (minimum).

subject: Electrical Engineering
question_text: The impedance Z of an RLC series circuit is:
explanation: Z=√(R²+(XL−XC)²). At resonance XL=XC so Z=R (minimum impedance, maximum current).
